sections.paragraph:
ὑπερεγώ is the way Lachmann writes ὑπὲρ ἐγώ 2 Cor 11:23; s. B-D-F §116, 3 on related forms; cp. ὑπέρ C.

Lachmann Lachmann = KLachmann. This abbreviation used when no other editors of the Gk. NT are mentioned.

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1032
